description: Add a third recovery rung (Sonnet→Opus before Opus→Opus[1M]) to the loop engine's PromptTooLong handler, plus diagnostics: per-section byte breakdown in PromptResult, prompt dumps to .task-mgr/overflow-dumps/, structured JSONL event log at .task-mgr/overflow-events.jsonl, banner annotation when a task is mid-recovery, and corrected stderr messages.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: All scoped tests pass (per-iteration); full `cargo test` passes at milestones
No breaking changes to existing public APIs (PromptResult and IterationContext extensions are additive only)
Filesystem failures (disk full, permission errors) in the overflow module log warnings via eprintln! and do NOT propagate — observability is best-effort, recovery is not
Order of operations in PromptTooLong arm: ctx update → DB UPDATE → stderr → dump (best-effort) → JSONL (best-effort) → rotate (best-effort)
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at milestones — milestones must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Banner annotation inferred from model_overrides (must use overflow_recovered HashSet — see learning #893)
Unsanitized task_id flowing into dump filenames (path traversal risk)
DB UPDATE that runs AFTER dump/JSONL writes (recovery state must be durable before observability)
.unwrap() on filesystem operations in the overflow module (warnings via eprintln!, never propagate)
Effort downgrade below 'high' (the ladder floor; preserves the model.rs:42-48 invariant)

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: All current readers of ctx.model_overrides re-confirmed via grep (engine.rs:1761 expected)
All current readers of ctx.effort_overrides re-confirmed via grep (engine.rs:1778 expected)
All current readers of effective_model re-confirmed via grep
Banner construction site re-confirmed (engine.rs ~1793) and any other call sites listed
Crash escalation channels (check_crash_escalation, escalate_task_model_if_needed) re-confirmed as separate from model_overrides
Any drift from the PRD's Consumer Impact Table documented in the progress file
userStories.description: The PRD's Section 6 already contains a Consumer Impact Table for the PromptTooLong recovery branch (engine.rs:2044-2113). This task verifies the table is still accurate against current main and surfaces any new consumers added since the PRD was drafted on 2026-05-04. Document findings in the progress file under ANALYSIS-001.
userStories.notes: Reference: PRD section 6 "Consumers of Changed Behavior" and "Semantic Distinctions". Learning #893 (separate crash escalation from retry escalation) is the design invariant this task validates.
qualityDimensions: Grep with literal terms (model_overrides, effort_overrides, effective_model) — do not skim by visual inspection
Document file:line references in the progress file so downstream FEAT tasks can verify against the same anchors
Note any crash-escalation paths that have started writing to model_overrides (would invalidate the dedicated overflow_recovered marker design)
userStories.title: Refresh Consumer Impact analysis for the PromptTooLong branch

acceptanceCriteria: Test: escalate_below_opus(Some("claude-haiku-4-5-20251001")) returns Some(SONNET_MODEL)
Test: escalate_below_opus(Some("claude-sonnet-4-6")) returns Some(OPUS_MODEL)
Test: escalate_below_opus(Some("claude-opus-4-7")) returns None (already at ceiling)
Test: escalate_below_opus(Some("claude-opus-4-7[1m]")) returns None (1M variant counts as Opus, no further escalation)
Test: escalate_below_opus(Some("gpt-4")) returns None (unknown tier)
Test: escalate_below_opus(None) returns None
Tests use OPUS_MODEL / SONNET_MODEL / HAIKU_MODEL constants from model.rs, never literal strings (regression test no_hardcoded_models.rs would catch literals)
Known-bad discriminator: a stub returning OPUS_MODEL unconditionally must fail at least the Opus and unknown-tier cases
userStories.description: TDD tests for the new model.rs::escalate_below_opus(model) helper. Tests must be specific enough to reject a naive stub that returns Some(OPUS_MODEL) for everything.
edgeCases: Empty string input (Some("")) — expected: None (model_tier returns Default for empty)
Whitespace-only input (Some("  ")) — expected: None
Case variants (Some("SONNET"), Some("sOnNeT")) — expected: Some(OPUS_MODEL) (model_tier is case-insensitive substring)
1M Opus variant (Some("claude-opus-4-7[1m]")) — expected: None

failureModes.cause: model_tier substring matching false-positives (e.g., "opusoidal" model name)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Returns None per current model_tier logic; this test does not need to fix model_tier

invariants: Returns Option<&'static str> matching the style of downgrade_effort and to_1m_model
Never returns Haiku (the function only escalates UP)
userStories.notes: Reference: PRD FR-001. Existing tests for downgrade_effort (model.rs:941+) and to_1m_model (model.rs:1097+) are the style template. Six explicit test functions, one per tier input.
qualityDimensions: Each test names a single tier transition; do not bundle multiple tier inputs into one test (clear failure attribution)
Match the existing test naming pattern (test_<function>_<case>) seen in model.rs:947+
Use #[test] attributes; do not use #[ignore] — tests should pass once FEAT-001 lands
userStories.title: Initial tests for escalate_below_opus helper

acceptanceCriteria: Test: sanitize_id_for_filename("FOO-BAR_baz.42") returns "FOO-BAR_baz.42" (allowlist passthrough)
Test: sanitize_id_for_filename("FOO/BAR..baz") returns "FOO-BAR--baz" (slashes and double-dots replaced)
Test: sanitize_id_for_filename("") returns "_" (empty-input placeholder, never produces an empty filename component)
Test: sanitize_id_for_filename("../../../etc/passwd") returns a string with NO `/` and NO `..` substring
Test: sanitize_id_for_filename("task with spaces") returns "task-with-spaces"
Test: sanitize_id_for_filename("\0null\0byte") returns a string with NO `\0` byte
Known-bad discriminator: a stub returning the input unchanged must fail at least 4 of these cases
userStories.description: TDD tests for overflow::sanitize_id_for_filename(id) — the path-traversal defense for dump filenames.
edgeCases: Path traversal attempt ("../../etc/passwd") — expected: no path components escape
Empty string — expected: "_" (placeholder)
Pure special chars ("///") — expected: "---" or similar; no empty result
NUL byte injection — expected: replaced
Unicode chars ("tâsk") — expected: replaced (allowlist is ASCII-only)
invariants: Output is never empty (minimum 1 character; empty input → "_")
Function is total (never panics, no Result type)
userStories.notes: FEAT-002 implements the function. Reference: src/loop_engine/worktree.rs:24-33 for the established sanitization pattern. Learning #1853: "Worktree paths sanitize branch names".
qualityDimensions: Mirror the worktree.rs::sanitize_branch_name allowlist style (learning #1853)
Test file lives at src/loop_engine/overflow.rs in a #[cfg(test)] mod tests block
ASCII allowlist — no need to support Unicode since dump filenames are diagnostics, not user-visible
userStories.title: Initial tests for sanitize_id_for_filename

acceptanceCriteria: Test: OverflowEvent serializes to a single JSON object with snake_case keys (ts, task_id, run_id, iteration, model, effort, prompt_bytes, sections, dropped_sections, recovery, dump_path)
Test: RecoveryAction::DowngradeEffort { new_effort } serializes to {"action": "downgrade_effort", "new_effort": "high"}
Test: RecoveryAction::EscalateModel { new_model } serializes to {"action": "escalate_model", "new_model": "claude-opus-4-7"}
Test: RecoveryAction::To1mModel { new_model } serializes to {"action": "to_1m_model", "new_model": "claude-opus-4-7[1m]"}
Test: RecoveryAction::Blocked serializes to {"action": "blocked"} with no extra fields
Test: round-trip — serde_json::from_str(&serde_json::to_string(&event)?) reconstructs an equal OverflowEvent
Test: sections field serializes as an array of [name, size] pairs in declaration order (NOT a map; order matters for the dump header)
Test: run_id is Option<String>; None serializes as null OR is skipped via #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
userStories.description: TDD tests for OverflowEvent and RecoveryAction serde behavior — ensure JSONL schema is stable and snake_case.
edgeCases: Empty sections vector — serializes as [] not null
Empty dropped_sections vector — serializes as []
run_id: None — either skipped or null, but never serializes as empty string
Effort: None — same Option handling as run_id

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Tests asserting "action": "downgrade_effort" fail; CamelCase variant names leak into JSON

invariants: snake_case key names across the entire event tree (RecoveryAction included)
sections preserves Vec ordering through serialization (positional [name, size] tuples)
userStories.notes: FEAT-002 implements OverflowEvent and RecoveryAction. RecoveryAction is a tagged enum: #[serde(tag = "action", rename_all = "snake_case")]. Reference: PRD FR-004 example JSONL line.
qualityDimensions: Use serde_json::Value to assert key names rather than substring grep on the serialized string (less brittle)
Construct OverflowEvent with realistic field values from the PRD's example (ca83bc7f-TEST-001, claude-sonnet-4-6, etc.)
Test exists in the same #[cfg(test)] mod tests block as TEST-INIT-002 since both target overflow.rs
userStories.title: Initial tests for OverflowEvent JSON serialization

acceptanceCriteria: Test: ladder walk — starting Sonnet+xhigh, four synthetic PromptTooLong events produce [downgrade_effort → escalate_model → to_1m_model → blocked] in order
Test: explicit-Opus skip — task with model='claude-opus-4-7' set in DB hits to_1m_model on first overflow (escalate_below_opus returns None)
Test: filename sanitization — task_id 'FOO/BAR..baz' produces a dump at .task-mgr/overflow-dumps/FOO-BAR--baz-iter1-*.txt with no path traversal
Test: dump content — each dump file exists, header includes total bytes + section breakdown + auto-load NOTE, body is the assembled prompt verbatim
Test: JSONL append — each iteration appends one parseable line to .task-mgr/overflow-events.jsonl with recovery.action matching the rung
Test: rotation — after ≥4 overflows on one task, .task-mgr/overflow-dumps/ contains exactly 3 files for that task (newest by mtime)
Test: overflow_recovered — after iter 1, ctx.overflow_recovered.contains(&task_id) is true
Test: overflow_original_model — captured on first overflow only; banner annotation across iters 2/3/4 still says 'from claude-sonnet-4-6'
Test: rung 4 (blocked) writes BOTH dump and JSONL with recovery.action='blocked'
Tests use isolated tempdir for .task-mgr/ — no pollution of the real project state
Tests use real CrashType::PromptTooLong / IterationContext / OverflowEvent types from the production crate (no synthetic key-typed maps)
userStories.description: TDD scaffold for tests/overflow_recovery.rs. Tests use direct outcome injection: a synthetic IterationOutcome::Crash(CrashType::PromptTooLong) feeds the recovery helper without orchestrating Claude spawn. Walks the full ladder + explicit-Opus skip + sanitization + rotation.
edgeCases: Tempdir cleanup on test failure (use tempfile::TempDir for RAII)
Iteration counter advances correctly between synthetic overflows
Concurrent test execution doesn't share tempdir (each test gets its own)
Test runs deterministically across platforms (mtime resolution can be coarse on some FS — use sleep(10ms) between rotation events if needed)

failureModes.cause: Test pollutes real .task-mgr/ instead of tempdir
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test must use tempfile::TempDir and pass an explicit dir parameter to overflow:: helpers; reject any test that writes to ./.task-mgr

failureModes.cause: Synthetic IterationContext built with hand-typed keys instead of using real struct
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Tests must construct via IterationContext::new() and mutate real fields; test must fail to compile if struct shape changes (catches drift)

invariants: All test assertions reference real ctx fields (overflow_recovered, overflow_original_model) and real types — NEVER hand-built maps with stringly-typed keys
Production code path is exercised end-to-end (no skipping the actual fs writes via mocks)
Tests do NOT spawn Claude (direct outcome injection is the seam)
userStories.notes: FEAT-005 will factor the recovery logic into a testable helper function so this test can call it directly. Per resolved Q8 (PRD section 7), the helper signature should be roughly: pub(crate) fn handle_prompt_too_long(ctx: &mut IterationContext, conn: &Connection, task_id: &str, effort: Option<&str>, effective_model: Option<&str>, prompt_result: &PromptResult, iteration: u32, run_id: Option<&str>, base_dir: &Path) -> RecoveryAction. Tests pass an isolated tempdir as base_dir.
qualityDimensions: Tests run via `cargo test --test overflow_recovery` independently of the rest of the suite
Each test has a single, named scenario — no umbrella 'test_everything' assertions
Use tempfile::TempDir for filesystem isolation (RAII cleanup even on panic)
Use the production CrashType enum from src/loop_engine/config.rs (variant: PromptTooLong)
userStories.title: Integration test scaffold: four-rung overflow ladder

acceptanceCriteria: Positive test: when ctx.overflow_recovered.contains(&task_id) AND ctx.overflow_original_model.get(&task_id) returns Some("claude-sonnet-4-6"), the banner string includes "(overflow recovery from claude-sonnet-4-6)"

Negative test: when ctx.model_overrides has an entry for task_id BUT ctx.overflow_recovered does NOT, the banner string does NOT include "overflow recovery" (prevents false-positive from future non-overflow writers to model_overrides)
No-op test: when neither overflow_recovered nor model_overrides has the task, the banner is the existing format unchanged
Test: banner with overflow_original_model = None for task (shouldn't happen but defensive) — either omits the annotation or uses a fallback like "overflow recovery (original model unknown)"
Tests use the real IterationContext and the real banner-formatting function (not a re-implementation in test code)
userStories.description: TDD tests for the iteration banner annotation. Critically includes the negative test that prevents banner false-positives if a future code path writes to model_overrides for non-overflow reasons.
edgeCases: Task in overflow_recovered but missing from overflow_original_model (race or programmer error) — expected: graceful fallback, no panic
Multiple tasks recovering simultaneously (overflow_recovered.len() > 1) — banner only annotates the current iteration's task
Iteration banner for a task that completed recovery and is now succeeding — annotation persists (override is for the loop's lifetime, per resolved Q4)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Negative test fails: banner shows 'overflow recovery' for a model_overrides entry written by a non-overflow path

invariants: Annotation never appears unless overflow_recovered.contains(&task_id) is true
Annotation source is overflow_original_model, never inferred from current model_overrides value
userStories.notes: FEAT-006 implements both the extraction and the annotation. Reference: PRD FR-006 banner string format. Critical: the negative test is the BLOCKER-1 fix from the architect review.
qualityDimensions: Tests live in the engine.rs #[cfg(test)] mod tests block

If banner formatting is currently inline in run_iteration, FEAT-006 must extract it into a pure formatter function (e.g. format_iteration_banner_with_recovery) so the test can call it directly without orchestrating an iteration
userStories.title: Initial tests for banner annotation gating

acceptanceCriteria: Function signature exactly: pub fn escalate_below_opus(model: Option<&str>) -> Option<&'static str>
Implementation uses model_tier() for tier classification (case-insensitive substring)
Returns SONNET_MODEL constant for Haiku tier; OPUS_MODEL constant for Sonnet tier (no string literals)

All TEST-INIT-001 tests pass
cargo clippy -- -D warnings on src/loop_engine/model.rs is clean

userStories.notes: Reference: PRD FR-001 and the plan's escalate_below_opus snippet. Learning #851: follow existing escalation patterns for consistency. The helper is a small, focused addition — one function, ~10 lines, no new types.
qualityDimensions: Mirror the style of downgrade_effort and to_1m_model: match expression, &'static str return, no allocations
Use SONNET_MODEL and OPUS_MODEL constants — the regression test no_hardcoded_models.rs will catch literal strings outside model.rs
Add the function near downgrade_effort and to_1m_model so all overflow-recovery primitives are co-located
Document the invariant: 'effort is NOT touched by this function; preserves the high floor for overflow recovery'

acceptanceCriteria: File created at src/loop_engine/overflow.rs
Module registered in src/loop_engine/mod.rs (`pub(crate) mod overflow;` or `pub mod overflow;` matching existing module visibility)
OverflowEvent struct: derives Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize; #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]; fields per PRD FR-004

RecoveryAction enum: derives Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize; #[serde(tag = "action", rename_all = "snake_case")]; variants DowngradeEffort{new_effort}, EscalateModel{new_model}, To1mModel{new_model}, Blocked
sanitize_id_for_filename(id: &str) -> String: ASCII allowlist [A-Za-z0-9._-], others replaced with '-', empty input returns "_"

dump_prompt(dir: &Path, task_id: &str, iter: u32, header: &DumpHeader, prompt: &str) -> io::Result<PathBuf>: creates dir if missing, writes <sanitized>-iter<N>-<unix-ts>.txt, returns the absolute path
DumpHeader struct: task_id, iteration, model: Option<String>, effort: Option<String>, ts_iso8601, total_bytes, sections: &[(&str, usize)], dropped_sections: &[String]
append_event_log(dir: &Path, event: &OverflowEvent) -> io::Result<()>: opens with OpenOptions::new().append(true).create(true), single write_all of serde_json::to_vec(&event)? + b'\n'
rotate_dumps_keep_n(dir: &Path, sanitized_task_id: &str, keep: usize) -> io::Result<()>: lists files matching <sanitized_task_id>-iter*-*.txt, sorts by mtime desc, deletes from index `keep` onward

format_breakdown(sections: &[(&str, usize)], dropped: &[String], total_bytes: usize) -> String: produces the dump header (multi-line, with the auto-load NOTE explaining Claude's CLAUDE.md/skills/agents auto-load layer)
All filesystem errors propagate as io::Result; callers (engine.rs) downgrade them to eprintln warnings
All TEST-INIT-002 and TEST-INIT-003 tests pass
userStories.description: New module hosting OverflowEvent, RecoveryAction, sanitize_id_for_filename, dump_prompt, append_event_log, rotate_dumps_keep_n, format_breakdown. Pure-fs helpers + serializable types; NO ctx mutation, NO DB access (those live in engine.rs::handle_prompt_too_long which composes these).
userStories.notes: Pure module: types + filesystem helpers. NO ctx, NO DB. The orchestration that mutates IterationContext + runs DB UPDATE lives in engine.rs::handle_prompt_too_long (FEAT-005). This split keeps overflow.rs unit-testable in isolation and engine.rs can mock the helpers via direct calls. Reference: PRD FR-002, FR-003, FR-004, FR-005.
qualityDimensions: No .unwrap() on filesystem operations — use ? operator or explicit match
No unsafe code
No allocations in sanitize_id_for_filename hot path beyond the single output String
format_breakdown produces deterministic output (sections rendered in input Vec order) so dump headers are reproducible
DumpHeader uses Option for model and effort so the (default) / None case is explicit at the type level (per resolved Q2)
append_event_log uses serde_json::to_vec NOT to_string — saves a UTF-8 validation pass
userStories.title: Create src/loop_engine/overflow.rs module with types and helpers

acceptanceCriteria: PromptResult struct (prompt.rs:51) gains `pub section_sizes: Vec<(&'static str, usize)>` field
Field documented with rustdoc comment explaining purpose and ordering invariant (preserves assembly order)

build_prompt populates section_sizes as it assembles each named section: task, task_ops, completion, escalation, reorder_instr, base_prompt, learnings, source, dependencies, synergy, siblings, steering, session_guidance, reorder_hint
Section names are static string slices (&'static str) — no allocations per iteration
All PromptResult construction sites updated, including the early-return / test fixture site at prompt.rs:847 (initialize as Vec::new())
Sanity-check test: section_sizes.iter().map(|(_, n)| n).sum::<usize>() <= TOTAL_PROMPT_BUDGET (80_000) for a representative invocation
cargo test prompt::tests passes
cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean
userStories.description: Extend PromptResult with `pub section_sizes: Vec<(&'static str, usize)>` (parallel to the existing dropped_sections field). Populate inside build_prompt as each named *_section: String is finalized.
userStories.notes: Reference: PRD FR-002. Existing precedent: dropped_sections at prompt.rs:69. Construction sites to update: prompt.rs:389 (main path), prompt.rs:847 (early-return). Search for `PromptResult {` to find all sites. Learning #522: incremental field addition pattern.
qualityDimensions: Use &'static str for section names (no String allocations)
Vec preserves insertion order (per resolved Q5) — NEVER replace with HashMap or BTreeMap
Populate ONLY for sections that made it into the final prompt; sections dropped for budget already record in dropped_sections
Field is additive — no behavior change for current consumers (learning #522 pattern)
If a section is empty (zero bytes), still record (name, 0) so callers can distinguish 'absent' from 'dropped' (which goes into dropped_sections)

IterationContext struct gains: pub overflow_original_model: HashMap<String, String>
Both fields initialized to empty in IterationContext::new() (no functional change to existing behavior)

Rustdoc on each field: overflow_recovered explains the dedicated-marker rationale (not inferred from model_overrides); overflow_original_model documents the entry().or_insert() (first-overflow-only) capture rule
All test fixtures and direct constructions of IterationContext updated (grep `IterationContext {` and `IterationContext::new`)
cargo build clean
cargo test loop_engine::engine passes
cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean
userStories.description: Add two new fields to IterationContext (engine.rs ~line 203 area): overflow_recovered: HashSet<String> and overflow_original_model: HashMap<String, String>. Initialize both as empty in IterationContext::new(). Used by FEAT-005 (recovery logic) and FEAT-006 (banner annotation).
userStories.notes: Reference: PRD FR-006 and the resolved Q6 (capture only on first overflow per task). Learnings: #1832 (IterationContext pattern), #1861 (per-slot, not thread-safe), #106 (cross-iteration state precedent).
qualityDimensions: Match existing IterationContext field documentation style (learning #1832)
Use std::collections::HashSet and HashMap (already imported in engine.rs)
Field visibility: pub (matches existing model_overrides / effort_overrides at engine.rs:203, 208)
Document the first-overflow-only capture rule on overflow_original_model so future maintainers know to use entry().or_insert() not insert()
userStories.title: Add overflow_recovered and overflow_original_model fields to IterationContext

acceptanceCriteria: Recovery branch walks rungs in order: downgrade_effort → escalate_below_opus → to_1m_model → block. Rung 2 inserts into ctx.model_overrides; rungs 2/3 do NOT touch ctx.effort_overrides (preserves high floor); rung 1 inserts into effort_overrides only.
All four rungs (including blocked) populate ctx.overflow_recovered (HashSet insert) and ctx.overflow_original_model (entry().or_insert() — first-overflow-only capture per resolved Q6)
Stderr messages match PRD FR-007 phrasings exactly, using effective_model.as_deref().unwrap_or("(default)") so "None" never appears (learning #209)

Recovery body factored into pub(crate) fn handle_prompt_too_long(ctx, conn, task_id, effort, effective_model, prompt_result, iteration, run_id, base_dir) -> RecoveryAction so integration tests can call it directly

Order of operations within handle_prompt_too_long: (1) pick rung + ctx mutation, (2) DB UPDATE (status='todo' on rungs 1-3 with started_at=NULL, 'blocked' on rung 4), (3) eprintln stderr message, (4) dump_prompt (best-effort), (5) append_event_log (best-effort), (6) rotate_dumps_keep_n (best-effort, keep=3)
Filesystem failures (steps 4-6) log eprintln warnings and return the RecoveryAction unchanged — do NOT propagate, do NOT fail the iteration
Zero .unwrap() / .expect() in handle_prompt_too_long
TEST-INIT-001 tests pass (helper function tests)
TEST-INIT-004 tests pass (integration ladder + sanitization + rotation + skip-rung-2 + blocked observability)
ANALYSIS-001 has passes=true (verifies the consumer impact assumptions before this implementation lands)

consumers.mitigation: Rung 2 inserts into the same map exactly like rung 3 — reader is unchanged
consumers.usage: Reads ctx.model_overrides.get(&task_id) when computing effective_model for next iteration

consumers.mitigation: Rung 1 inserts into this map; rungs 2/3/4 do not. No new write site.
consumers.usage: Reads ctx.effort_overrides.get(&task_id) when computing effort for next iteration

consumers.mitigation: FEAT-006 extends banner to read overflow_recovered + overflow_original_model. Negative test in TEST-INIT-005 prevents false-positive.
consumers.usage: Iteration banner formatting reads model field

consumers.mitigation: Separate channel — does not touch model_overrides. Learning #893 documents the invariant.
Separate channel — DB-level escalation, not in-memory override. No conflict with overflow recovery.

semanticDistinctions.currentBehavior: Rung 1 then rung 3, then block. Sonnet+high → blocked
semanticDistinctions.requiredBehavior: Rung 1 → rung 2 (new) → rung 3 → block. Sonnet+high → Opus+high → Opus[1M]+high → blocked

semanticDistinctions.currentBehavior: Returns Option<String> from local function; flows into effective_model directly
semanticDistinctions.requiredBehavior: Unchanged — stays in its own channel, does NOT write to model_overrides

semanticDistinctions.currentBehavior: Updates DB tasks.model column based on consecutive failures

userStories.description: Refactor the PromptTooLong arm in engine.rs (~lines 2044-2113) to walk three recovery rungs (downgrade_effort → escalate_below_opus → to_1m_model) before blocking. Factor the body into pub(crate) fn handle_prompt_too_long so tests can call it directly. Modifies behavior — depends on ANALYSIS-001.
userStories.notes: Data contract: RecoveryAction (overflow.rs) → OverflowEvent (overflow.rs) → JSONL line. Source fields: ts (chrono), task_id (sanitized), run_id (loop's run_id, Option<String>, may be None), iteration (u32, the loop counter), model (Option<String>, effort_used: Option<String>), prompt_bytes (prompt_result.prompt.len()), sections (Vec<(&'static str, usize)> from prompt_result.section_sizes), dropped_sections (Vec<String> from prompt_result.dropped_sections), recovery (RecoveryAction enum), dump_path (PathBuf returned from dump_prompt). Access pattern: serde_json::to_vec(&event)?; file.write_all(&line)?. The loop's run_id is threaded through engine.rs as a String parameter — verify it's still in scope at the PromptTooLong arm before calling handle_prompt_too_long.
qualityDimensions: Function length: handle_prompt_too_long can exceed 30 lines per learning #1370 (linear pipeline orchestrator). Stay under 80.
All branches return a RecoveryAction so callers can introspect what happened
No .unwrap() on conn.execute results — match Ok(1) / Ok(_) / Err(e) explicitly (existing pattern in this branch)
Effort downgrade preserves the model; model escalation preserves the effort — invariants from learning #1856
Override persistence is for the loop's lifetime (resolved Q4) — never clear ctx.model_overrides / overflow_recovered after a successful iteration
When the helper is called from the existing PromptTooLong arm, all current side effects (DB UPDATE, stderr) must continue to fire — do not silently drop them
userStories.title: Three-rung recovery in PromptTooLong branch + corrected stderr messages

acceptanceCriteria: Banner construction is extracted into a pure function format_iteration_banner_with_recovery(...) that takes model, effort, iteration, total_iterations, task_id, elapsed, ctx_overflow_recovered: &HashSet<String>, ctx_overflow_original_model: &HashMap<String, String>
When overflow_recovered.contains(task_id) AND overflow_original_model has the task, banner Model field becomes: 'Model: <current> (overflow recovery from <original>)'

When overflow_recovered.contains(task_id) but overflow_original_model is missing the entry, banner gracefully falls back (no panic, no 'None' literal in output) — e.g., '(overflow recovery)' without the 'from X' suffix
When neither overflow_recovered nor model_overrides has the task, banner is the existing format unchanged
TEST-INIT-005 tests pass (positive, negative, no-op, missing-original-model)
cargo test loop_engine::engine clean
cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean
userStories.description: Update the iteration banner construction (engine.rs ~line 1793) to append '(overflow recovery from <original-model>)' when ctx.overflow_recovered.contains(&task_id). Read the original model from ctx.overflow_original_model.
userStories.notes: Reference: PRD FR-006. The format extraction is what makes TEST-INIT-005 testable; without it the banner is built inline and tests would have to spin up an entire iteration. Architect's BLOCKER-1: do NOT gate the annotation on model_overrides; use the dedicated overflow_recovered HashSet.
qualityDimensions: Banner format function is pure (no I/O, deterministic output) so it's unit-testable without orchestrating an iteration
Annotation source is overflow_original_model, NEVER inferred from current model_overrides value (BLOCKER-1 fix)
Use &str / &HashSet / &HashMap parameters — no need to clone for the formatter
userStories.title: Banner annotation when overflow recovery is active

acceptanceCriteria: Wiring check: `grep -n 'pub mod overflow' src/loop_engine/mod.rs` returns a hit
Wiring check: handle_prompt_too_long is invoked from the PromptTooLong arm (no orphan helper)
Wiring check: format_iteration_banner_with_recovery is called from the iteration banner construction site (no orphan formatter)
Quality check: ZERO .unwrap() / .expect() in src/loop_engine/overflow.rs production code (test code allowed)
Quality check: ZERO .unwrap() / .expect() in handle_prompt_too_long
Order check: in handle_prompt_too_long, ctx mutations precede DB UPDATE, which precedes stderr, which precedes filesystem ops
Sanitization check: no path that writes to overflow-dumps/ uses raw task_id; all routes go through sanitize_id_for_filename
Banner gating check: format_iteration_banner_with_recovery reads overflow_recovered HashSet, NOT model_overrides
Effort floor check: rung 2 (escalate_below_opus path) does NOT touch ctx.effort_overrides
Any issues found have corresponding CODE-FIX-xxx or WIRE-FIX-xxx tasks added via task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by MILESTONE-1
userStories.description: Review all FEAT-001 through FEAT-006 changes for: no .unwrap() in production paths, qualityDimensions satisfied, wiring reachability (overflow.rs registered in mod.rs, OverflowEvent reachable from engine.rs, format_iteration_banner_with_recovery called from the banner site), no banner false-positive (gated on overflow_recovered, not model_overrides), correct order of operations in handle_prompt_too_long.
userStories.notes: For each issue: `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by MILESTONE-1`. Use rootCause / exactFix / verifyCommand fields on spawned tasks so the implementing agent lands the fix in one pass.
qualityDimensions: Use the rust-python-code-reviewer agent for this review
Spawn one fix task per issue — do NOT batch unrelated issues into one task
If no issues, emit `<task-status>CODE-REVIEW-1:done</task-status>` with a one-line progress note 'No issues found'

acceptanceCriteria: Test: rotation_keeps_newest_3 — create 5 dumps with controlled mtimes (use std::fs::set_modified or ms-resolution sleeps), assert exactly 3 newest remain
Test: rotation_no_op_when_under_limit — with 2 dumps, rotate keeps both
Test: rotation_only_touches_matching_task — with dumps for task A and task B, rotating A leaves B untouched
Test: jsonl_append_preserves_existing_lines — pre-seed file with 3 lines, append, assert all 4 lines parseable

Test: jsonl_append_atomic_for_large_event — OverflowEvent with 50 sections + 5KB total serialization writes as a single line (no interleaving with simulated concurrent writer is in scope only if cheap; otherwise just assert single-line for the 5KB case)
Test: format_breakdown contains all expected substrings: 'Total assembled bytes:', 'Section breakdown:', 'NOTE:', 'auto-loaded layer'
Test: dump_prompt header reflects sanitized task_id (not raw input)
All tests use tempdir; no pollution of real .task-mgr/
cargo test --test overflow_filesystem (or whatever module name is used) passes
`cargo test` full suite still clean
userStories.description: Beyond the TEST-INIT scaffolding, add tests for: rotation correctness with edge mtime resolution, JSONL atomicity (single-line writes even for >4KB events), dump header formatting, sanitize round-trip on a corpus of inputs.
edgeCases: FS with second-level mtime resolution (rotation may need ms-level sleeps to disambiguate)
Empty sections vector in dump header
Long task_id (256 chars before sanitize) — filename should still be reasonable
Append to a file that doesn't exist yet (overflow-events.jsonl on first run) — must create with the right perms

failureModes.cause: Test relies on filesystem mtime ordering without sleeping
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Add 10ms sleep between dump writes to ensure mtime distinction

invariants: Rotation never deletes a file outside the dumps dir
JSONL append never truncates existing content
userStories.notes: These are post-MILESTONE-1 tests — the basic behavior is already covered by TEST-INIT scaffolding. This task hardens edge cases and concurrency-adjacent behavior.
qualityDimensions: Use tempfile::TempDir for filesystem isolation
Each test scopes to one specific behavior — no umbrella tests
Avoid wall-clock timing dependencies where possible; when unavoidable (mtime), use minimum sleeps and document why
userStories.title: Comprehensive tests for overflow.rs filesystem behavior

acceptanceCriteria: Test: override_persists_across_iterations — after rung 2 fires, ctx.model_overrides[task_id] still contains Opus on iter 5 even with no further overflows

Test: original_model_captured_first_overflow_only — task walks Sonnet+xhigh → Sonnet+high → Opus+high; ctx.overflow_original_model[task_id] is 'claude-sonnet-4-6' on all three observations (never updated to current model)
Test: run_id_none_serializes_correctly — with run_id=None, JSONL line either omits the field or shows null, NEVER empty string
Test: effective_model_none_dump_header — with effective_model=None, dump header shows 'model: (default)' (not 'model: None' or empty)
Test: rung_4_writes_observability — blocked task creates BOTH dump and JSONL with recovery.action='blocked'
Test: dump_uses_sent_effort_not_resolved — task at Sonnet+xhigh hits rung 1; dump header shows 'effort: xhigh' (the sent value), JSONL shows recovery.action='downgrade_effort' with new_effort='high'
All tests pass against handle_prompt_too_long with synthetic IterationContext + tempdir
Full `cargo test` clean
userStories.description: Beyond the TEST-INIT-004 ladder walk, add tests for: override persistence across iterations, overflow_original_model first-overflow-only capture, run_id None propagation through the JSONL, effective_model None producing 'model: (default)' in dump header.
edgeCases: Task_id with sanitization needed (e.g., 'TEAM/PROJ-001') — dump file uses sanitized form, JSONL preserves the raw task_id field
Iteration counter at u32 boundary (well into the millions) — file format still correct
Empty section_sizes (early-exit prompt build) — dump header shows zero sections, total_bytes still accurate

failureModes.cause: Implementation uses ctx.overflow_original_model.insert() instead of entry().or_insert()
failureModes.expectedBehavior: test_original_model_captured_first_overflow_only fails: original drifts to current model after each cascade

invariants: Override persistence: once set, ctx.model_overrides[task_id] is never cleared by recovery code
Original model: captured on first overflow only, stable across cascading recoveries
userStories.notes: Reference: resolved Q4 (override persistence), Q6 (first-overflow-only capture), Q7 (block-task observability), Q3 (sent-effort in dump).
qualityDimensions: Each test asserts ONE specific invariant — do not bundle multiple checks per test
Tests construct IterationContext via IterationContext::new() and mutate real fields, NEVER hand-built struct instances bypassing public API

acceptanceCriteria: Path traced and documented in progress file: detect (detection.rs::classify_crash) → dispatch (engine.rs PromptTooLong arm) → helper (engine.rs::handle_prompt_too_long) → fs (overflow.rs::dump_prompt + append_event_log + rotate_dumps_keep_n)
Handoff verified at each boundary by reading actual call sites (no inferred connections)
All function signatures at boundaries match the data contract in the prompt's Data Flow Contracts section
PromptResult.section_sizes is consumed correctly by handle_prompt_too_long (Vec is iterated in order, not converted to a map)
OverflowEvent fields populated from real loop state (run_id from Loop's run_id, iteration from Loop's counter, etc.) — no synthetic placeholders
If the trace reveals any wiring gap (e.g., helper exists but never called, struct field never read), spawn a WIRE-FIX-xxx via task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by MILESTONE-FINAL
If the trace is clean, emit `<task-status>INT-001:done</task-status>` with the trace summary in the progress file
userStories.description: Trace one complete overflow event through every module boundary: engine.rs (PromptTooLong detected) → handle_prompt_too_long (engine.rs) → overflow.rs helpers (sanitize, dump, append, rotate) → filesystem. Verify wiring is correct end-to-end and the assertions about the call graph hold.
userStories.notes: Cross-boundary INT task per /tasks Step 4.8. Path is engine.rs (top-level) → overflow.rs (helpers) → fs. Single trace covers the data path; no other INT tasks needed for this PRD.
qualityDimensions: Trace is grep-verified, not narrated. Each handoff lists file:line on both sides of the call.
